---
title: "Unrefereed Course Notes"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

note_entries <- c("Title", "Author", "Subject", "Pages", "University", "Contact", "Abstract")

note_data <- c("Graph Theory", "Benedict, James M.", "graph theory", "27", "Augusta State University", "jbenedic@aug.edu", "The discovery method, accredited to R. L. Moore, is the inspiration for the style of the presentation found within this text. The text is intended for undergraduates. It allows for a one-semester development of the most elementary yet universal concepts of Graph Theory. Topics include graph theoretic equality, degrees of vertices, subgraphs, complements of graphs, special subgraphs, the four-color theorem, planar graphs, the Petersen graph, and traceable graphs.")


tibble(note_entries, note_data) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_align(align = "left") |>
  tab_style(
    style= cell_text(v_align = "top"),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) |>
  tab_options( table.width = "80%", column_labels.hidden = TRUE)  


```
- [Download File](./graph-theory_benedict.pdf)

This course guide was donated to a collection originally gathered by MathNerds. The author retains copyright. You are free to use these materials for your classes.
